mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding household income and consumption demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward hit rate falls below 52 percent, or the top PAYEMS growth quintile fails to outperform the bottom quintile over a 2-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate of more than 0.25 percentage points over the prior three months signals an accelerating monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and credit costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds rate at 1.69% in April 2018 within hiking cycle
failure_condition: Triggered instances yield positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or the mean forward return when the trigger fires is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and rising policy rates (FEDFUNDS) indicate restrictive real monetary policy that erodes corporate margins and equity valuations, jointly predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered periods, positive 60-day returns occur in more than 50 percent of instances, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales (RSAFS) during a late-cycle expansion signal peaking consumption momentum and forward earnings downgrade risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances in late-cycle periods produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or the Rank IC in this regime is not negative and significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor-market slack and falling household confidence, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward hit rate of negative returns on trigger falls below 53 percent, or the mean 5-day forward return when the trigger fires is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) of more than 1 percent over two months signals negative supply momentum and weakening corporate output, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production index at 107.1665 in March 2018
failure_condition: Triggered instances yield positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the mean forward return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1,250 thousand units signals constrained construction activity and slowing mortgage demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases or the mean return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects rising capacity utilization and corporate revenue expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the top INDPRO growth quintile fails to outperform the bottom quintile.
